rationale: The citing paper references Mende et al. (2019) once in the conceptual background as an example that discomfort can trigger compensatory consumption (e.g., status spending and overeating). This appears as part of a broader literature list used to set up competing predictions, not as a specific theoretical or methodological pillar for the paper's own studies. The main mechanism and evidence in the citing paper center on anxiety-driven avoidance of public consumption after school shootings, with no direct extension or focused engagement with the service-robot framework.
